textPlain: CSRF: Cross-Site Request ForgeryFrom https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Attack...In a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attack, an attacker tricks the user or the browser into making an HTTP request to the target site from a malicious site. The request includes the user's credentials and causes the server to carry out some harmful action, thinking that the user intended it.

Are CSRF attacks that common nowadays though? Even if your app is used by the 5% of browsers that don’t set the Origin header the chances of that being exploited are even more miniscule. Besides, most webdevs reach for token-based auth libraries before even knowing how to set a cookie header.
Do most languages have good support for TLS 1.3 as the client?
